title: A Look At Lululemon Athletica (LULU) Valuation After Mixed Earnings And Founder Activism - simplywall.st
India eases rules to allow return of export ships amid war disruption
content:
India has eased customs rules for returned export cargo amid Strait of Hormuz disruptions.
The new Section 143AA framework allows faster clearance without Bill of Entry if seals are intact, simplifies documentation via SAM, and enables shipping bill cancellation.
Valid till April 30, 2026, it supports textile exporters while safeguarding incentives recovery.

title: Long energy disruptions to raise pressures on SEA nations: S&P Global
content:
Prolonged energy disruptions will raise fiscal and external pressures on Southeast Asian nations, according to S&P Global. 
Indonesia is more vulnerable to weakening credit metrics if the war continues and energy prices remain high. 
Vietnam’s strong economic growth, its booming export sector and relatively unencumbered government balance sheet will act as ballast against the energy market dislocation.

title: India’s polyester raw material stabilises after decline, PSF steady
content:
India’s polyester chain is entering a consolidation phase, with PTA and MEG stabilising after recent volatility and fibre prices holding steady amid weak demand.
Mixed global cues and easing geopolitical tensions are limiting sharp movements, though crude oil trends remain a key risk.
The market is likely to stay range-bound in the near term.

title: Iran war ups risks to growth, fiscal outlooks in DM sovereigns: Fitch
content:
A prolonged Middle East conflict could create new credit challenges for developed market (DM) sovereigns in Europe and Asia, primarily through higher energy and borrowing costs, rising inflation and weaker economic growth, according to Fitch Ratings. 
Sovereigns with higher debt and structural deficits, and those facing worse inflation-growth trade-offs, are more vulnerable to a sustained shock, it noted.

title: Should Cambodia recalibrate its focus amid evolving trade dynamics?
content:
As ASEAN+3 gains ground, there is a reported shift towards intra-regional demand, reducing reliance on Western markets and driving integrated regional growth. 
As per some experts, Cambodia stands to benefit from the same by catering to regional demand, backed by Regional Comprehensive Economic Partnership (RCEP), and growing investment inflows.

title: US fashion firms accelerate sourcing shift as supply chains falter
content: US fashion brands and retailers are increasingly shifting sourcing away from China, in favour of nearshoring and exploring supply options across wider Asia.

title: UNI welcomes Bangladesh labour law reform boosting union rights
